title: Victims Come Forward In Hopes To Ban Non-Disclosure Agreements | 10 News+
description: Three women who claim they were sexually harassed at work in three different workplaces came together to speak out on non-disclosure agreements, silencing victims.  -----------------------------------------------------  Corrective Services NSW spokesperson: “We have a zero-tolerance policy towards sexual assault and harassment in our workplaces. We recognise the strength and courage of victim-survivors who have experienced harm at the hands of these individuals. CSNSW are not involved in the process of NDAs for victims-survivors. We encourage any staff member who is aware of misconduct in the workplace to report it to Professional Standards and Investigations or to NSW Police so we can continue to identify and act upon instances of wrongdoing.”  Defence spokesperson: “Defence continues to work to prevent sexual violence, and acting on incidents sensitively, diligently and in a trauma-informed way. Where reports are received Defence takes action through a trauma-informed approach. Support is always available to our people. In circumstances where sexual violence has occurred or is alleged, Defence places considerable weight on the wishes of the claimant, including where the claimant wishes to maintain their privacy.”  Attorney-General Michelle Rowland spokesperson: “All Australian workplaces should be safe and free from sexual harassment. The Government’s policy is generally not to seek confidentiality in the settlement of workplace sexual assault or sexual harassment claims, and to only agree to confidentiality where this is requested by the claimant. Confidentiality will not be used to prevent a claimant from being able to tell their story. The upcoming statutory review of the Anti-Discrimination and Human Rights Legislation Amendment (Respect at Work) Act 2022 (Cth) may provide an opportunity to consider further the use of non-disclosure agreements in workplace sexual harassment cases.”   Join the conversation and follow us on: Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/10NewsPlus/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@10newsplus/ Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/10newsplus/ Snapchat: https://www.snapchat.com/add/tennewsplus Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/0tWZ6sIJTQ2WHgYdjgaAA7?si=cb30d7fd3d3541fa  10 News+ airs at 6pm on Monday to Friday and at 6:30pm on Sunday on Network 10. title: Trump Says The World Is "Lucky" To Have Him | 10 News
description: US President Donald Trump has again lavished praise on himself, saying the entire world should be thanking him for launching an unprovoked military assault on Iran.  Trump also stated Ireland, represented in the White House by Taoiseach Michael Martin, is "lucky" to have him, and that without his efforts to tear up a prior nuclear deal with Iran, the world would have experienced a "nuclear holocaust."  Trump also took aim at NATO allies, who were not consulted by the US prior to the launch of the Iranian military offensive, for not rushing to the US's aid in attempting to secure the crucial Strait of Hormuz. title: Punch The Monkey Finds A New Companion | 10 News
description: Punch the baby macaque, who went viral for clinging to a stuffed toy orangutan after being abandoned by his mother, has found a new companion at Japan’s Ichikawa City Zoo.  He’s now been spotted playing, cuddling and hanging out with an older macaque named Momo-chan after months of being alone.  Clips of the pair have taken off online, with fans loving the development, though experts say it’s more like a big-sister bond than romance.  Punch’s long-trusted plush toy still appears in a few clips, however it is no longer the sole object of his affection.   Zookeepers say Punch is mixing more with the group, a positive sign he’s settling in.
